seoTitle: Tincurrin vs Tom Price — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Tincurrin and Tom Price. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Tincurrin vs Tom Price: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tincurrin and Tom Price.
heading: Tincurrin vs Tom Price — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tincurrin, Western Australia and Tom Price, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tom Price has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Tincurrin (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tincurrin or Tom Price safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tincurrin has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Tom Price scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tincurrin scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Tom Price scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tincurrin or Tom Price?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tom Price has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
